channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Congested Reagan National site of several previous close calls
item.description: The complex airspace around Washington, D.C., has resulted in at least three near misses in the last three years.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Trump wants to cut the federal workforce. Who they are and what that means
item.description: President Trump’s efforts to cut the federal workforce by 10 percent might not save as much money as hoped and could have unintended consequences. NPR explains why.
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman, MPR News

item.title: Federal court rules ATF age limits on handgun sales violate Second Amendment
item.description: A three-judge panel of the 5th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals in New Orleans found a federal law that prohibits adults aged 18-20 from buying handguns violates the Second Amendment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump’s FCC chief opens investigation into NPR and PBS
item.description: Brendan Carr, the new head of the Federal Communications Commission under President Trump, says the public broadcasters are being investigated for allegedly running commercials.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: New FOIA on migrants potentially avoiding the draft could open new deportation predicate: attorneys
item.description: A Freedom of Information Act request from the Oversight Project may potentially open a new avenue for mass deportations, according to one lawyer involved.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's FBI chief pick, Kash Patel, insists he has no 'enemies list' and won't seek retribution
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Kash Patel, President Donald Trump’s pick to lead the FBI, insisted to deeply skeptical Democrats on Thursday that he did not have an “enemies list" and that the bureau under his leadership would not seek retribution against the president's adversaries or launch investigations for
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate advances Trump's energy secretary nominee to final confirmation vote
item.description: President Donald Trump's pick to head the Department of Energy, Chris Wright, has advanced to a final confirmation vote in the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans advance Trump OMB nominee as Democrats boycott vote
item.description: Senate Republicans advanced the nomination of Russell Vought to lead the White House budget office as Democrats boycotted the meeting to protest the administration's recent efforts to freeze large swaths of federal funding.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: GOP senator 'surprised' Gabbard refused to call Snowden a traitor
item.description: Sen. James Lankford (R-Okla.), a member of the Senate Intelligence Committee, said he was “surprised” that director of national intelligence nominee Tulsi Gabbard refused to call former NSA contractor Edward Snowden a “traitor” after being repeatedly pressed on the question at a Thursday hearing.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Top Republican on Senate health committee says he's 'struggling' to confirm RFK Jr
item.description: The top Republican on the Senate's chief health committee, Sen. Bill Cassidy, R-La., indicated Thursday that he was “struggling" to confirm Robert F. Kennedy Jr. over his inability to admit vaccines are safe and don't cause autism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Black Caucus blasts Trump's DEI comments after Potomac plane crash
item.description: "Not only are the President’s claims untrue, they also speak to the Republican Party’s desire to divide us as a country.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump warns Canada, Mexico tariffs are coming on Saturday
item.description: "We’ll be announcing the tariffs on Canada and Mexico for a number of reasons," Trump told reporters in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Senators grill 3 of Trump's most controversial Cabinet picks in confirmation hearings
item.description: Three of President Donald Trump’s cabinet picks faced skepticism and intense grilling from Democratic senators during their confirmation hearings on Thursday. What we’re following: 1. Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump was challenged after blaming DEI for the DC plane crash. Here's what he said
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump began his White House briefing Thursday with a moment of silence and a prayer for victims of Wednesday's crash at Reagan National Airport.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FCC chair launches investigation into NPR, PBS
item.description: The chairman of the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) is launching an investigation into NPR and PBS over their alleged "airing of commercials.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about military helicopter involved in crash near DC
item.description: A deadly collision on Wednesday night between an American Airlines plane with 60 passengers and four crew and an Army UH-60 Black Hawk with three soldiers remains under investigation amid questions about how the crash occurred.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Big Oil wants a lot from Trump. It has an ally in Doug Burgum, the president's Interior pick
item.description: Emails obtained by The Associated Press reveal Doug Burgum as North Dakota governor catered to the whims of oil and gas executives while leveraging those connections to expand his political profile.
item.dc:creator: Brian Slodysko And Jack Dura, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Patel dodges, demurs as Democrats press him on controversial statements
item.description: Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to lead the FBI, wouldn’t acknowledge or sought to distance himself from a host of his past public statements as Democrats pressed him during his confirmation hearing on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Watch live: Gabbard makes case to lead national intelligence at confirmation hearing
item.description: Former Hawaii Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (D-Hawaii) is testifying Thursday morning before the Senate Select Committee on Intelligence to make her case as President Trump's pick for director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Jackson punches out her frustrations with the conservative Supreme Court in the boxing ring
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — One of three liberals on a Supreme Court dominated by conservatives, Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson said she has found an outlet for the frustration that can result from being in the minority on the nation's highest court: boxing.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota ACLU: Local jurisdictions have the right to not cooperate with immigration enforcement
item.description: During President Donald Trump’s first week in office, his new Department of Justice announced plans to potentially criminally prosecute local and state officials who fail to “comply with lawful immigration-related commands and requests.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crann and Ngoc Bui, MPR News

item.title: Trump appeals hush money criminal conviction
item.description: President Trump appealed his New York hush money criminal conviction and has brought on a new team of attorneys to represent him.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez embraces role as attack dog against Trump
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez is embracing her role as one of the party's sharpest critics of President Trump, while other Democrats are working on a softer approach, with Ocasio-Cortez's actions showing her as the party's most aggressive member.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: Medicaid, SNAP, other benefits safe for Nevadans, Lombardo says
item.description: Gov. Joe Lombardo issued a statement Tuesday to clarify what is and is not affected by executive orders issued by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 5 takeaways from the first Trump White House briefing
item.description: President Trump’s press secretary Karoline Leavitt held her first White House briefing Tuesday, fielding questions from reporters for just more than 45 minutes.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5
